task_identity.title: Complete language-classified line-by-line review packets and reviewer assignments

preconditions: HEAD equals 9cf56e149a105026b072c9073d7e87b93103966e or an approved updated audit cut exists.
Every in-scope tracked/generated file is present in FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv and assigned to a named reviewer.
The current behavior, public claim tier, assumptions, affected consumers, and rollback are recorded before editing.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.
mandatory_counterfactuals: correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples
required_evidence: positive and negative vectors or fixtures
coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable
handoff_task_contract.completion_rule: No prose-only closure. Every acceptance condition and evidence item must pass on the exact candidate; otherwise mark NOT_CLAIMED or NO_GO.

twenty_lenses.name: Claims and scope
twenty_lenses.question: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.

twenty_lenses.name: First-principles semantics
twenty_lenses.question: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?

twenty_lenses.name: Mathematics and statistics
twenty_lenses.question: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?

twenty_lenses.name: Type and state integrity
twenty_lenses.question: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?

twenty_lenses.name: Time, ordering, and replay
twenty_lenses.question: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?

twenty_lenses.name: Identity and provenance
twenty_lenses.question: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?

twenty_lenses.name: Authentication and cryptography
twenty_lenses.question: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?

twenty_lenses.name: Authority and safety
twenty_lenses.question: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?

twenty_lenses.name: Hostile inputs and parsers
twenty_lenses.question: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?

twenty_lenses.name: Resource and denial-of-service bounds
twenty_lenses.question: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?

twenty_lenses.name: Concurrency and lifecycle
twenty_lenses.question: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?

twenty_lenses.name: Determinism and reproducibility
twenty_lenses.question: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?

twenty_lenses.name: API, FFI, and SemVer
twenty_lenses.question: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?

twenty_lenses.name: Schema, wire, and language parity
twenty_lenses.question: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?

twenty_lenses.name: Configuration and deployment
twenty_lenses.question: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?

twenty_lenses.name: Observability and forensics
twenty_lenses.question: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?

twenty_lenses.name: Verification and evidence quality
twenty_lenses.question: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?

twenty_lenses.name: Ecosystem composition
twenty_lenses.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?

twenty_lenses.name: Human factors and governance
twenty_lenses.question: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?

twenty_lenses.name: Counterfactual and quirky-case review
twenty_lenses.question: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

affected_surface_inventory.rationale: Generated exact-F classification, assignment, catalog, and subject-scope contract consumed by the product and independent lifecycle verifiers.
The immutable 356-row CH-T001 inventory is completed only in its frozen mutable review columns and remains independently reconstructed from Git objects.
Bounded deterministic packet builder and verifier for the exact signed-F tree, assignment partition, and completed ledger.
Frozen positive, negative, parser, classification, assignment, and lifecycle regression suite executed by qualification evidence.

normative_controls.statement: The signed F freeze SHALL have the exact registered schema, task identity, author, release target, and absent persistent identifier.
The signed F freeze SHALL declare exactly the four I implementation paths and the same exact verification-trigger path set.

The I overlay SHALL contain exactly 391 classification records, one for every regular Git blob at the signed F tree in RAW_UTF8_BYTE_ASC order; SHALL bind classification policy HASH_PINNED_INDEPENDENT_AUDIT_PLUS_EXPLICIT_REVIEW_OVERRIDES_AND_F_EXTENSION_V1, audit schema HALDIR_CH_T002_INDEPENDENT_CLASSIFICATION_AUDIT_V1, SHA-256 1222a6c9e6962a8c0ad6ac5196868084c03d230e0ba976baa8fe9b49be464441, current-A commit 2167b0b1b8580298b8474e676893f97292c3d7c7, current-A tree 66c906443dae3338117f8a43e740b9161811ef7a, override policy INDEPENDENT_AUTOMATED_PLUS_LOCAL_BYTE_REVIEW_OVERRIDES_V1, exactly 92 field overrides across exactly 74 paths in RAW_UTF8_BYTE_ASC_PATH_THEN_ASCII_FIELD_ASC order, haldir-ch-t002-classification-override-set-v1 digest 546a1e62fc78ef9f7921cfda1ce0cc3f75712c813565c69159fa353569ba32d6, NO_REMOVALS_PER_EXACT_BASELINE_TO_FREEZE_DIFF, the frozen generated and primary-capture semantics, and haldir-ch-t002-classification-set-v1 digest c146353dc97fe3e9b2b75a025e7686e8ff217070a0a4d6e6686f5f14e946fb33; and every ledger row, overlay entry, packet, and CH-T002-E09 decision SHALL agree exactly.
The completed ledger SHALL preserve every immutable CH-T001 field and SHALL resolve every mutable review field under the frozen classification decisions.
The I overlay SHALL contain the exact derived requirement, test, evidence, and generator catalogs and exact implementation boundary.
CH-T002 SHALL reject every removal because the exact baseline-to-F delta contains no deleted path; removed_paths SHALL be empty.
Every F subject SHALL receive exactly one deterministic primary assignment and every critical subject SHALL receive one distinct secondary assignment.
Every packet subject SHALL bind the complete Git blob byte interval and exact text line interval, including an empty text blob.
Every ledger evidence pointer SHALL be canonical, typed, and resolvable through the frozen evidence catalog.
The F freeze SHALL require exactly E01 through E13, four review records, and A01 through A05 with their exact kinds, paths, and byte bounds.
The registered Python test artifact SHALL parse as bounded Python syntax and expose every exact test identifier declared by the F freeze.
The freeze SHALL retain the exact ten-second verifier budget and SHALL NOT permit a caller to relax the hard budget.
The three independent automated lanes and automated non-human lead support SHALL use four distinct registered machine-operated, non-human, non-external principals, public keys, and fingerprints.
Each review acceptance SHALL carry a valid Ed25519 SSH signature bound to its registered principal, namespace, exact F commit, and exact I commit.
All protocol JSON outside the F pretty freeze and central pretty registry SHALL be finite, duplicate-free, bounded canonical JSON.
Every protocol path SHALL be NFC, repository-relative, portable, and outside Git administrative paths.
A03 retained run and attempt-job API records SHALL bind exact endpoints, headers, tool version, capture times, bytes, and SHA-256 digests.
A05 SHALL be a bounded safe ZIP with one nonempty primary per exact CI job name and unique contiguous provider ordinals zero through five.
Every activation command SHALL bind its exact phase, argv, working directory, ordered time window, nonempty output, exit code, and stream digests.

CH-T002 SHALL remain NO_GO with no public claim change; only deterministic packet generation, assignment mechanics, and automated technical qualification may qualify; source human-review coverage SHALL remain NOT_CLAIMED and external human review DEFERRED; and CH-T002 SHALL authorize no tag, GitHub release, DOI, Zenodo record, or archive.

mandatory_counterfactuals.statement: correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples

combined_attack_matrix.statement: valid signature plus stale epoch or wrong route
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Lifecycle and SSH-signature bindings reject stale commits, principals, namespaces, and routes.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: authenticated peer plus oversized payload and expensive logging
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: All parsed artifacts, streams, execution time, and retained archives are bounded before acceptance.
Canonical syntax is insufficient without exact classification, ledger, catalog, and packet agreement.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: green unit tests plus divergent generated binding
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Exact classification and generated-source bindings are checked independently of a green product test result.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: nominal statistic plus missing lifecycle/projection evidence
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: CH-T002 makes no population statistic, calibration, or lifecycle-projection claim.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A CH-T002 outcome begins asserting a population statistic, calibration result, or lifecycle projection.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: ALLOW then crash before/after publication and restart
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: CH-T002 emits no runtime ALLOW decision and grants no publication operation or authority.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A CH-T002 interface begins issuing runtime ALLOW decisions or publishing authority-bearing output.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: matching package version plus wrong algorithm/contract digest
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Version labels cannot substitute for the exact audit, override, classification-set, and registered-program digests.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: timeout or optional-component loss plus convenience fallback
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Deadline or capacity exhaustion terminates the bounded verifier without cached, partial, or convenience fallback.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: correct local ACL file plus wrong real router
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: CH-T002 changes offline review artifacts only and neither installs nor validates an ACL or live router.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A CH-T002 implementation path begins configuring or qualifying a live ACL or router.

combined_attack_matrix.rationale: The exact 391-blob signed-F distribution is reconstructed; a clean subset cannot satisfy complete scope.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: canonical payload plus noncanonical signing bytes
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Review signatures bind canonical namespaces, principals, commits, and the exact retained review payload.
The four-path implementation adds offline review tooling and cannot activate a runtime privileged adapter.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A feature or adapter in the CH-T002 implementation diff becomes a runtime or privileged product path.

combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Every mutable ledger field must be explicitly resolved; unknown or defaulted partial migration is rejected.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: clock rollback plus apparently valid TTL/lease
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Retained evidence has chronology, but CH-T002 accepts no TTL, lease, or clock-derived runtime authority.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A CH-T002 interface begins accepting a TTL, lease, or freshness decision derived from a clock.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: cancellation request plus already-selected event and duplicate effect
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: Packet generation is a bounded offline derivation and exposes no asynchronous cancellation or selected-event effect.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: The packet interface begins accepting cancellation after selecting a side effect or permits duplicate external effects.

handoff_command_mapping.rationale: The signed updated audit cut and exact prior active-claims record supersede the stale source hash.
The signed CH-T001 inventory and independent reconciliation are immutable prerequisites to this review scope.
The replacement binds exact Git objects, classifications, deterministic assignments, complete byte or line intervals, and a packet manifest.
CH-T003 owns public-surface and claim-tier inventory after the CH-T002 technical review packet is activated.

threat_model.asset_or_claim: exact classification contract and complete signed-F scope
threat_model.preconditions: ability to alter an audit pin, override row, classification decision, or scope count
threat_model.sequence: substitute one classification or omit one F blob while retaining a plausible aggregate
threat_model.trust_boundary: independent source audit and explicit override layer versus generated overlay
threat_model.observable_symptoms: audit, override-set, classification-set, count, or path-set mismatch
threat_model.worst_consequence: a relevant file is assigned under the wrong criticality or excluded from review
threat_model.recovery: stop qualification, restore exact pinned classifications, regenerate the overlay, and repeat every affected review packet
threat_model.residual_risk: classification remains a bounded technical judgment and does not establish named-human review
threat_model.claim_impact: technical qualification fails on any scope or classification drift

threat_model.preconditions: a byte or line interval is omitted, duplicated, reordered, or assigned twice
threat_model.sequence: emit balanced-looking packets whose subjects do not reconstruct every exact Git blob
threat_model.trust_boundary: Git object bytes versus packet subject intervals and manifest
threat_model.observable_symptoms: subject, interval, assignment, or packet-set digest mismatch
threat_model.worst_consequence: review completion is recorded despite an unread interval
threat_model.recovery: reject all packets, repair the deterministic derivation, and reproduce the complete packet set
threat_model.residual_risk: semantic understanding is not inferred from structural interval completeness
threat_model.claim_impact: no complete technical review packet claim survives an interval mismatch

threat_model.preconditions: access to an unregistered key, stale signed payload, or duplicated reviewer principal
threat_model.sequence: present a valid SSH signature under the wrong namespace, key, principal, F commit, or I commit
threat_model.trust_boundary: four keys frozen in signed F versus later review records
threat_model.observable_symptoms: principal, fingerprint, key, namespace, commit, or separation mismatch
threat_model.worst_consequence: an unauthorized record is treated as technical review acceptance
threat_model.recovery: reject qualification, revoke the affected packet, register a new epoch, and repeat the review
threat_model.residual_risk: local key separation does not establish real-world identity, organizational independence, or named-human review
threat_model.claim_impact: only registered automated technical review is accepted

threat_model.preconditions: automated evidence is available without a named-human or external review record
threat_model.sequence: describe automated line and context review as human, external, release, deployment, or publication approval
threat_model.trust_boundary: bounded technical automation versus real-world review and release authority
threat_model.observable_symptoms: claim outcome or qualification boundary contradicts NO_GO and the explicit limitation
threat_model.worst_consequence: users infer assurance or authorization that the evidence does not support
threat_model.recovery: remove the overclaim, retain NO_GO, and obtain separately identified review before any stronger disposition
threat_model.residual_risk: later prose outside the signed lifecycle can still overstate the evidence and requires renewed review
threat_model.claim_impact: human and external review remain explicitly not claimed

threat_model.preconditions: ability to delete a baseline path or insert a synthetic removal marker
threat_model.sequence: hide a deleted file behind a tombstone or removed-path exception while claiming full review
threat_model.trust_boundary: exact A-to-F Git diff versus overlay removal representation
threat_model.observable_symptoms: deleted path, nonempty removed_paths, tombstone, or baseline mismatch
threat_model.worst_consequence: unreviewed removal changes behavior while appearing covered
threat_model.recovery: reject the epoch and either restore the path or freeze a new explicit implementation contract
threat_model.residual_risk: later task epochs must independently bind their own deletions
threat_model.claim_impact: this epoch accepts no deletion or tombstone semantics

threat_model.actor: oversized repository input or resource exhaustion
threat_model.preconditions: an input reaches a frozen byte, path, stream, archive, or time limit
threat_model.sequence: exhaust a limit and request cached, partial, skipped, or best-effort review fallback
threat_model.trust_boundary: bounded verifier acceptance versus convenience behavior
threat_model.observable_symptoms: capacity or deadline failure with no success receipt
threat_model.worst_consequence: partial coverage is treated as complete
threat_model.recovery: reduce the exact candidate within frozen limits or open a new epoch; never reuse partial output
threat_model.residual_risk: successful operation at every hard ceiling is not claimed
threat_model.claim_impact: only exact-candidate measurements and fail-closed limits are qualified

threat_model.preconditions: ability to alter provider ordinals, job names, member paths, compression metadata, or CRC values
threat_model.sequence: insert traversal, aliases, duplicate primaries, sparse ordinals, or unrelated job output
threat_model.trust_boundary: GitHub run and job API records versus bounded retained ZIP bytes
threat_model.observable_symptoms: archive structure, provider ordinal, job set, CRC, size, method, or digest mismatch
threat_model.worst_consequence: unrelated or incomplete logs are attributed to the exact hosted run
threat_model.recovery: discard the archive and recapture exact run, attempt, jobs, API records, and raw log ZIP
threat_model.residual_risk: retained provider metadata is not a cryptographic attestation by GitHub
threat_model.claim_impact: hosted evidence fails if the bounded archive is not exact

threat_model.preconditions: ability to substitute a worktree, stale commit, merge, or adjacent lookalike record
threat_model.sequence: present matching content from the wrong lifecycle identity
threat_model.trust_boundary: signed first-parent commits versus mutable worktree and alternate history
threat_model.observable_symptoms: subject, parent, diff, signature, tree, or evidence identity mismatch
threat_model.worst_consequence: bytes outside the frozen lifecycle are technically qualified
threat_model.recovery: reject the candidate and reconstruct evidence solely from exact signed commit objects
threat_model.residual_risk: Git object-store and signer compromise are outside repository-local proof
threat_model.claim_impact: qualification is bound only to the exact signed lifecycle

threat_model.preconditions: product code and its tests share one faulty interpretation
threat_model.sequence: produce internally consistent packets and green product tests that violate the frozen contract
threat_model.trust_boundary: product implementation versus independently registered lifecycle verifier
threat_model.observable_symptoms: differential classification, catalog, assignment, or packet reconstruction mismatch
threat_model.worst_consequence: correlated false acceptance of incomplete or misclassified review
threat_model.recovery: reject qualification and repair both implementations against Git objects and the hash-pinned audit
threat_model.residual_risk: shared standard-library or Git defects remain trusted dependencies
threat_model.claim_impact: the product helper is not its own sole qualification oracle

threat_model.asset_or_claim: canonical portable protocol paths and hostile-input handling
threat_model.preconditions: control over a path or structured record supplied to a review interface
threat_model.sequence: use Unicode ambiguity, traversal, duplicate keys, unsafe nesting, or contradictory metadata
threat_model.trust_boundary: untrusted structured bytes versus bounded canonical parsers
threat_model.observable_symptoms: canonical path, duplicate-key, depth, size, or semantic contradiction rejection
threat_model.worst_consequence: a record resolves to a different repository subject or bypasses review
threat_model.recovery: reject the record, preserve the exact failing bytes, and regenerate only canonical protocol data
threat_model.residual_risk: unknown future encodings require a new protocol version
threat_model.claim_impact: noncanonical or ambiguous inputs cannot satisfy technical review coverage

misuse_resistant_interfaces.justification: The overlay is the single retained mapping from exact signed-F blobs to classifications, assignments, catalogs, and packet scope.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.correct_example: Bind all 391 regular F blobs in raw-byte path order to the exact audit, override set, classifications, counts, and no-removal policy.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.wrong_example: Accept matching counts while omitting a path, changing one override, or inserting a tombstone.
non_proofs: classification and assignment records do not establish named-human or external review
misuse_resistant_interfaces.evidence_tier: exact signed-tree bounded technical classification and assignment verification

misuse_resistant_interfaces.justification: The operator-facing generator derives complete deterministic lane packets and must not silently narrow scope or recover through fallback.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.correct_example: Generate from the exact F commit, completed ledger, and overlay, then retain every packet and manifest identity.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.wrong_example: Generate from current worktree bytes, skip an oversized subject, or continue after a classification mismatch.
non_proofs: packet generation is not runtime validation, deployment qualification, or release approval

misuse_resistant_interfaces.justification: Verification can be misused against a plausible later tree or incomplete evidence instead of exact signed lifecycle objects.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.correct_example: Verify exact F and I commits, canonical overlay and ledger bytes, complete packet intervals, and all catalog pointers.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.wrong_example: Treat a clean current worktree, matching row count, or schema version as proof of the exact F and I scope.
non_proofs: valid syntax and clean worktree state are not exact lifecycle identity
misuse_resistant_interfaces.evidence_tier: exact-commit product verification with independent registered reconciliation

misuse_resistant_interfaces.justification: The central runner invokes this bounded verifier as an independent acceptance boundary for F-I-C-D evidence and review records.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.correct_example: Run with exact signed commits under the frozen ten-second budget and accept only the exact typed receipt.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.wrong_example: Relax the deadline, use an unregistered reviewer, substitute a stale commit, or infer release authority from PASS.
non_proofs: a passing automated verifier is not named-human review, external approval, field validation, or release authority

limitations: Automated line-by-line and relevant-context review does not constitute completed named-human or external human review.
Classification is a bounded technical record for the exact signed F tree and does not establish runtime product correctness.
Hosted workflow metadata and logs are retained byte-bound records, not cryptographic attestations issued by GitHub.

LIMITATION (CH-T002/E07): requirements_complete=true attests frozen CH-T002 technical and evidence requirements only: deterministic complete byte/line packet coverage of every regular Git blob on the exact signed snapshot; primary-lane assignment for every subject and distinct secondary-lane assignment for every critical subject; three independent signed automated technical review records plus signed automated lead-support review under distinct frozen keys. Human review coverage is NOT performed and NOT claimed: automated_review_only=true; independent_human_review_performed=false; named_human_review_performed=false; required_external_human_review_satisfied=null. No human, named-human, external, organizational, release, or publication review or approval is asserted or implied. Overall release status remains NO_GO; named/external human review is deferred to designated successor tasks.
No release, deployment, publication, tag, GitHub release, DOI, Zenodo record, or archive authority is granted.
Successful operation at hard resource ceilings is not claimed; limits are fail-closed validation bounds.
